breadcrumbTrail: Introduction to Brain Neoplasms and Cysts
anki.deckName: RadprimerNormal::All Categories::Neuro::Brain::Introduction to Brain Neoplasms and Cysts::Colloid Cyst
deckParts: Introduction to Brain Neoplasms and Cysts

imageRegistry.caption: Axial graphic shows a classic colloid cyst (CC) at the foramen of Monro causing mild/moderate obstructive hydrocephalus. Note that the fornices and choroid plexus are elevated and stretched over the cyst .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial gross pathology in a patient who suddenly and inexplicably died shows a large CC causing moderate obstructive hydrocephalus. A small cavum septi pellucidi is present. Fornices are draped over the cyst. (Courtesy R. Hewlett, MD.)

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT in a 45-year-old man presenting with headaches demonstrates a classic CC, seen as a hyperdense mass wedged into the foramen of Monro and upper 3rd ventricle. Note that there is no dilatation of the lateral ventricles.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Sagittal T1 MR shows a classic T1-hyperintense CC along the anterior and superior roof of the 3rd ventricle. A majority of CCs are T1 hyperintense due to viscous fluid and cholesterol.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1W MR in a 52-year-old man with headache, amnesia, and transient ischemic attack (TIA)-like symptoms shows a large, mildly hyperintense, well-delineated mass in the anterior 3rd ventricle at the foramen of Monro. Note the blood-fluid level in the dependent part of the mass.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial FLAIR MR shows the cyst is extremely hyperintense and does not suppress. Note hyperintensity in the fornices and adjacent corpus callosum genu suggesting edema from acute inflammation.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial FLAIR MR in a 24-year-old man who presented with severe headaches shows severe obstructive hydrocephalus with dilated lateral ventricles and interstitial edema . A well-defined FLAIR hyperintense mass is present at the foramen of Monro.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial DWI image shows no restricted diffusion . Although CCs usually have viscid gelatinous material, they do not show restricted diffusion. ADC values are often higher than that of normal cerebral parenchyma and lower than CSF.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal T2 MR in the same patient demonstrates a T2-hyperintense mass with an eccentric T2-hypointense intracystic nodule . Intracystic nodule dot sign has been described in CC similar to a Rathke cleft cyst. T2 hypointensity is likely due to free radical-containing compounds. CCs and Rathke cleft cysts are endodermal in origin.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Sagittal T1 C+ MR in the same patient shows very subtle rim enhancement of the CC with an intracystic nodule .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT in a 16-year-old boy who presented in the ER with severe headaches and papilledema shows severe obstructive hydrocephalus with dilated lateral ventricles and complete effacement of all superficial sulci. An isodense mass is present at the foramen of Monro.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial FLAIR MR in the same patient shows the lesion to be very hyperintense and straddled by the fornix . Ventricles are dilated, and transependymal CSF flow is present. The CC was removed at surgery.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 MR shows a slightly hypointense CC . Note the pillars of fornix elevated and draped over the cyst.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT shows a round, hyperdense foramen of Monro mass causing mild hydrocephalus. Note the fornices draped and splayed around the mass. This is a classic CC.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T2 MR shows a CC at the foramen of Monro. The cyst is isointense with the brain and is causing moderate, but compensated hydrocephalus.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial FLAIR MR in the same patient shows that the cyst does not suppress and is hyperintense to brain. Note the absence of transependymal CSF flow around the atria of the lateral ventricle.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 MR shows a large, lobulated foramen of Monro mass in a middle-aged patient with headache and obstructive hydrocephalus.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T2 MR in the same patient shows a mixed signal mass with a focus of profound hypointensity ("black hole" effect) . An unusually large, inspissated viscous CC was found at surgery.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 MR shows a classic CC at the foramen of Monro. This patient had only mild headaches. Observation, rather than surgical intervention, was the chosen treatment.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 MR in the same patient, obtained after the patient developed a sudden increase in headaches, shows marked interval enlargement of the mass. The CC now appears less hyperintense, and hydrocephalus is present. The CC was removed at surgery.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Sagittal T1 MR shows a tiny CC discovered incidentally in this asymptomatic patient.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT in a 24-year-old man with severe headaches shows a hyperdense mass at the foramen of Monro. The superficial sulci are effaced, and the brain appears "tight," suggesting moderate cerebral edema.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 MR in the same patient shows the mass is slightly hyperintense compared to the brain.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T2 MR in the same patient shows most of the cyst appears relatively isointense with gray matter but exhibits a central hypointense focus that suggests inspissated colloid within the cyst.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal T2 MR shows the central inspissated cyst as very hypointense. Note elevation and splaying of the fornices around the cyst.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 C+ SPGR MR for stereotaxic surgery shows enhancement around the lateral margins of the cyst. This represents displaced internal cerebral and septal veins, not rim enhancement. An inspissated CC was removed at surgery.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T2 MR in the same patient shows the mass is cystic and even more hyperintense than CSF in the adjacent ventricles. Note the blood-fluid level and splaying of the fornices around the mass.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 C+ FS MR shows rim enhancement around the cyst wall.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal T1 C+ MR in the same patient shows the rim enhancement surrounds the entire cyst wall. Diagnosis of a CC with apoplexy, intracystic hemorrhage, and inflammatory changes was documented at surgery.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT obtained to look for a subarachnoid hemorrhage in a 65-year-old man with a thunderclap headache shows a classic CC, seen here as a hyperdense mass wedged into the foramen of Monro and upper 3rd ventricle.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Sagittal T2 MR in the same patient shows the mass to be very hypointense, indicating inspissated proteinaceous contents. Note the markedly enlarged lateral ventricle with a normal-sized 3rd ventricle. The CC was removed emergently.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageEvidence.purpose: Visual evidence for duplicate detection. Use these image files before deciding that source images are exact duplicates.
imageEvidence.duplicateClassificationRule: Archive only images proven to be exact/same-slice/same-screenshot duplicates or unusable. Caption-only similarity is not enough; uncertain or merely similar images stay primary as recognition reinforcement.
